regions.text: Some desires are stronger than others. This baby sees the cat and he sees his ball. The cat has an attraction for him. He has a desire for the cat.
His ball has an attraction for him. He has a desire for the ball. Which will he go to? If his desire for the cat is stronger than his desire for the ball, he will go to the cat.
He went to the cat. His desire for the cat was stronger.
All our desires are for things which seem good to us. They may not be good. Our ideas of them may be wrong.
